description: Trigger-posture suite for the agentic-development skill. The suite checks whether the skill activates for explicit agent-system design work, stays manual-first, prefers minimal viable agency, handles ambiguous near-miss requests honestly, and routes prompt-only, non-agentic architecture, deterministic automation, and already-chosen implementation planning work to adjacent workflows instead.

assertion_definitions.routing_verdict_correct: The response makes the right top-level call: route into agentic-development for explicit agent-system design work, and redirect adjacent non-agentic work elsewhere.
assertion_definitions.invocation_posture_explicit: For routed requests, the response explicitly names the skill posture as `manual-first`.
assertion_definitions.posture_matches_request: Because this is a heavy, cross-cutting methodology skill, the response should keep the posture conservative and manual-first.
assertion_definitions.minimal_agency_bias_visible: The response shows a bias toward the smallest useful level of agency rather than defaulting to multi-agent architecture.
assertion_definitions.adjacent_boundary_respected: For near-miss or out-of-scope requests, the response explains the better neighboring workflow instead of stretching the skill boundary.
assertion_definitions.stage_routing_honest: The response chooses the smallest sensible next stage such as framing, framework selection, or route-to-planning instead of a generic everything-at-once answer.
assertion_definitions.next_step_explicit: The response ends with a concrete next step, artifact, or downstream workflow.

cases.name: Explicit tool-using agent design request should trigger
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Design an internal support agent that can search docs, draft answers, and open tickets with human approval for risky actions. I need the agent architecture, tool boundaries, and eval plan.' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill, what invocation posture applies, and what the next stages are. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is explicitly about designing an agentic system, so it should route to the skill.

posture_matches_request.check: The skill itself should remain manual-first because it is heavy and cross-cutting.

minimal_agency_bias_visible.check: The answer should mention choosing the smallest useful agency level rather than assuming a team of agents.

stage_routing_honest.check: The next stages should center on framing the operating contract and system architecture, not immediate implementation.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name the next artifact or step.

cases.name: Explicit framework choice for agent system should trigger
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Help me choose between LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, Google ADK, and Pydantic AI for a long-running Python agent system with human approvals and eval requirements.' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill, what posture applies, and what the next stages are. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is explicit agent-system framework selection, so it should route to the skill.

minimal_agency_bias_visible.check: The answer should keep pattern and operating contract ahead of framework fandom.

stage_routing_honest.check: The next stage should involve operating-contract framing and framework comparison, with fresh research only if current facts materially matter.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name a concrete next step.

cases.name: Workflow-vs-agent decision should trigger with minimal-agency bias
cases.prompt: A user says: 'I am not sure whether this product should use a workflow, a single agent, or a multi-agent system. Help me decide before we build anything.' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill, what posture applies, and what the next stages are. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is a direct suitability question for an agentic system, so it should route to the skill.

minimal_agency_bias_visible.check: The answer should make minimal viable agency central to the next step.

stage_routing_honest.check: The route should begin with suitability and pattern selection rather than framework choice or implementation.

cases.name: Prompt-only tuning should not trigger
cases.prompt: A user says: 'My system prompt keeps drifting from the JSON schema. Redesign the prompt and examples so the outputs stay valid.' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to agentic-development.

adjacent_boundary_respected.check: The answer should route to prompt-engineering instead of inflating the task into agent architecture.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should point to the better workflow.

cases.name: General non-agentic architecture should not trigger
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Help me choose the architecture for a multi-tenant analytics platform with Kafka, ClickHouse, and object storage.' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is architecture work, but not agentic-system work, so it should not route to this skill.

adjacent_boundary_respected.check: The answer should route to future-aware-architecture.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name the better next workflow.

cases.name: Deterministic automation should not default to agentic development
cases.prompt: A user says: 'I need a script that renames files based on a fixed naming convention and moves them into dated folders.' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to the skill because deterministic code is enough.

adjacent_boundary_respected.check: The answer should say a script or ordinary implementation flow is the lighter and better option.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should point to a direct implementation path.

cases.name: Already-chosen agent architecture should route to planning
cases.prompt: A user says: 'We already chose a LangGraph coordinator with specialist workers and approval checkpoints. Now write the phased implementation and rollout plan.' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill and why or why not. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not stay in agentic-development because the architecture choice is already made.

adjacent_boundary_respected.check: The answer should route to implementation-planning.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name implementation-planning as the next step.

cases.name: Broad AI-features request should trigger through minimal-shape framing
cases.prompt: A user says: 'I want to add AI features to my SaaS app, but I am not sure whether I need prompts, RAG, workflows, or agents yet.' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill, what posture applies, and what the next stages are. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should route to the skill because the real decision is whether the product needs prompts, retrieval, workflow automation, or true agency at all.

minimal_agency_bias_visible.check: The answer should make clear that the next step is to decide the smallest useful AI shape rather than assume an agent system.

stage_routing_honest.check: The next stages should focus on clarifying the product job and running a suitability decision before framework or implementation talk.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name a concrete framing or suitability step.

cases.name: MCP-only question can trigger, but must stay protocol-first and architecture-honest
cases.prompt: A user says: 'How should I use MCP in my project?' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill, what posture applies, and what the next stages are. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This can route to the skill because MCP is a protocol choice that only makes sense in the context of tools, orchestration, and the surrounding system design.

adjacent_boundary_respected.check: The answer should make clear that MCP is a protocol or connectivity layer, not a complete orchestration strategy.

stage_routing_honest.check: The next stages should clarify the role MCP plays in the system before choosing broader framework or runtime direction.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name a concrete next step such as defining the tool boundary or protocol role.

cases.name: Chatbot with RAG and tools can trigger, but only through a minimal-agency decision
cases.prompt: A user says: 'Help me build a chatbot with RAG and a few tools for internal users.' Using the agentic-development skill, answer in one short paragraph whether this should route to the skill, what posture applies, and what the next stages are. Do not edit files.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This can route to the skill because the boundary between plain RAG and a tool-using agent is exactly what needs to be decided.

minimal_agency_bias_visible.check: The answer should frame the next step as deciding whether a simple retrieval workflow is enough before recommending a richer agent pattern.

stage_routing_honest.check: The answer should start with suitability and operating-contract framing, not assume a multi-agent build.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should name a concrete next step or artifact.
